specificity.note: Names the exact node (cust.operator-large, 0.40 weight), the procurement instruments (AVLs, frameworks, reference-install prerequisites), named operators for monitoring (Equinix, NTT, Vantage), and a concrete perturbation (exclude the cell for the Year-1 window). Indicators carry numeric thresholds (≥2 of first 5 RFQs, >70% of first 10 conversations).

nonObviousness.note: Goes beyond 'the 0.40 might be soft' by naming a NEW structural reason: procurement machinery no ledger node represents, plus the finding that the 0.40 rests on one supplier's order mix, not a channel split. A founder knows AVLs exist, so full foreclosure-as-framing is sharp but the general 'big buyers are hard to sell into' skeleton is on most competent lists.

mechanismDepth.note: Three-step chain with refs at each hop (AVL prerequisites → cannot enter tenders → cell converts to zero in-window), and each link is independently plausible. The leap from 'qualification barriers exist' to 'structurally closed for the entire window' is the one arrow a hostile partner can push on, and the rationale itself concedes it is partly inferential.

evidenceQuality.note: Multiple independent sources corroborate the key premises: framework procurement (Find a Tender award), hyperscaler spec regimes (OCP/Olympus), and the OEM/white-label reference class (Moduledge), with the likelihood rationale honestly flagging that full-window foreclosure was not directly evidenced. Falls short of 4 because the load-bearing 'foreclosed, not slow' claim has no direct source and the falsifier was not tested.

decisionRelevance.note: Directly changes next-two-quarter sequencing: the strategic mitigation re-routes Year-1 GTM through OEM/integrator channels and demotes operator AVL entry to Year 2. Both indicators (RFQ prequalification clauses, CRM channel mix of first 10 conversations) are monitorable now with stated thresholds.
